title: Inception and Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Inception and Evangelion             Quote Post by ehh123 » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:21 pm I recently watched Inception recently and it got me to thinking. If we were to enter the dreams of Evangelion characters, what would we see? I know that I would probably be stuck aboard that subway Shinji is always on.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:38 pm You do realize that there is a theory that states that the world of NGE IS the imagination/fabricated reality of a adolescent Shinji Ikari as he uses that fabrication to deal with his issues in the real world, right?

[]: Re: Inception and Evangelion             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:38 pm ehh123 wrote: If we were to enter the dreams of Evangelion characters, what would we see?

[]: Re: Inception and Evangelion             Quote Post by Eric Blair » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:40 pm ehh123 wrote: If we were to enter the dreams of Evangelion characters, what would we see?

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jul 18, 2010 9:48 pm -

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 18, 2010 10:12 pm Clover wrote: Don't squander such a good comparison of material on this. There were huge parallels between Inception and Eva on the subject of reality.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:03 pm NemZ wrote: What, because of 26? I'd have to say eva goes much further than inception in far less time. The movie is interesting but doesn't seem to have all that much to say, really.

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:08 pm -

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:26 pm Clover wrote: Did you not notice the parts about (stuff)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:26 pm Inception of Evangelion... who's going to write the fanfic?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:31 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Inception of Evangelion... who's going to write the fanfic?

[]:             Quote Post by berto » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:39 pm OH, Sweet christmas, NO!!! I saw the movie, too, doesn't mean I want to blend that and this. I'm also watching the Waterboy doesn't mean I'm going to write a fic replacing the characters with the cast of Eva. I get the fact that one is more compatible than the other but it's the pricipal of the thing!!!

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Jul 18, 2010 11:43 pm berto wrote: I'm also watching the Waterboy doesn't mean I'm going to write a fic replacing the characters with the cast of Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jul 19, 2010 1:48 am The concept of Dreams vs Reality is not an entirely uncommon theme throughout fiction, and is particularly present within dystopian settings. Heck, even Wal-e had those kinds of elements. Seeing similarities between Eva and Inception isn't much of a stretch because the concepts aren't entirely original in their own merit. Then again, I've yet to see Inception. No spoilers, please~

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Jul 19, 2010 7:52 am Sachi wrote: Seeing similarities between Eva and Inception isn't much of a stretch because the concepts aren't entirely original in their own merit.

[]:             Quote Post by Holy Diver » Mon Jul 19, 2010 9:17 am Sachi wrote: Then again, I've yet to see Inception. No spoilers, please~

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Mon Jul 19, 2010 2:08 pm Oh good i wasn't the only one who thought about the subject of Inception and Evangelion, i agree, evangelion goes way deeper than inception. But for a CGI film that movie kick-ass it really was a good movie, i think a lot of us think of Evangelion when watching this movie is the "psychological "sci-fi" feeling you get from the movie. I wouldn't say they are exactly alike but they have one or two similarities, nonetheless i would definitely recommend this movie to anyone who like evangelion!

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Mon Jul 19, 2010 3:08 pm Idk how much Eva and Inception have in common, if anything at all, but the theme of Inception reminded me a lot of the Cowboy Bebop Movie. Watch it and see.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Jul 19, 2010 4:31 pm I'm tired of seeing everything that has even the slightest mindscrew element compared to Evangelion .

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Mon Jul 19, 2010 5:48 pm NAveryW wrote: I'm tired of seeing everything that has even the slightest mindscrew element compared to Evangelion .
